plugins.name: instruction-management
plugins.description: Tools to maintain and improve project instruction files (AGENTS.md and legacy CLAUDE.md) — audit quality, capture session learnings, and keep project instructions current.

plugins.name: agent-doublecheck
plugins.description: Three-layer verification pipeline for AI output. Extracts claims, finds sources, and flags hallucination risks so humans can verify before acting.

plugins.name: agent-ember
plugins.description: An AI partner, not a tool. Ember carries fire from person to person — helping humans discover that AI partnership isn't something you learn, it's something you find.

plugins.name: agent-eyeball
plugins.description: Document analysis with inline source screenshots. Generates a Word doc where every factual claim includes a highlighted screenshot from the source material for visual verification.

plugins.name: agentic-eval
plugins.description: Patterns and techniques for evaluating and improving AI agent outputs — self-critique loops, evaluator-optimizer pipelines, rubric-based scoring, and iterative refinement.

plugins.name: github-toolkit
plugins.description: Scaffold GitHub repo metadata — issue templates and CI workflows. /github-scaffold picks the task; /create-issue-template and /scaffold-ci-workflow run the subtasks.

plugins.name: agency-toolkit
plugins.description: Local fork of Anthropic's plugin-dev toolkit — skills, agents, and commands for building Claude Code plugins.

plugins.name: context-engineering
plugins.description: Memory and context engineering concepts for LLMs — context window management, RAG patterns, prompt injection attacks (red-team), and defenses. Covers three angles: supporting development, exploiting, and defending.

plugins.name: make-a-monorepo
plugins.description: Scaffold a flat monorepo from scratch or audit an existing project against monorepo conventions.

plugins.name: brainstorming
plugins.description: Turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through collaborative dialogue before any creative or implementation work begins.

plugins.name: grill-with-docs
plugins.description: A relentless interview to sharpen a plan or design, which also creates docs (ADRs and glossary) as it goes.

plugins.name: improve-codebase-architecture
plugins.description: Scan a codebase for deepening opportunities, present them as a visual HTML report, then grill through whichever one you pick.

plugins.name: systematic-debugging
plugins.description: Systematic root-cause debugging process to use before proposing fixes for any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior.

plugins.name: plan-execution
plugins.description: Load a written implementation plan, review it critically, execute all tasks, and report when complete.

plugins.name: dispatch-delegation
plugins.description: Dispatch independent tasks to parallel subagents with isolated context when facing 2+ tasks with no shared state or sequential dependencies.

plugins.name: writing-plans
plugins.description: Write comprehensive implementation plans for a spec or requirements, assuming zero codebase context, before touching code.

plugins.name: writing-skills
plugins.description: Create, edit, and verify skills using a TDD-style process: write pressure-test scenarios, watch them fail, write the skill, verify compliance.

plugins.name: hooks-toolkit
plugins.description: Composable safety and hygiene hooks — force-push guard, secret scanner, plugin/skill manifest validators, branch nudges, and dirty-tree stop check.

plugins.name: ludus-toolkit
plugins.description: Ludus cyber-range toolkit — skills for CLI, range config, environment setup, and troubleshooting, plus a bundled MCP server for the Ludus API.

plugins.name: docker-toolkit
plugins.description: Build and validate Docker artifacts — multi-stage Dockerfiles, and packaging MCP servers as containers runnable via the Docker MCP Gateway.
